sourceMovie.title: Once Upon a Time in Mexico
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Once Upon a Time in Mexico (2003) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Sicario
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Once Upon a Time in Mexico clicked for you, Sicario (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Once Upon a Time in Mexico clicked for you, The Equalizer 2 (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Once Upon a Time in Mexico?
faq.answer: Try Sicario, The Equalizer 2, Sicario: Day of the Soldado—each shares DNA with Once Upon a Time in Mexico while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Once Upon a Time in Mexico?
faq.answer: Once Upon a Time in Mexico (2003) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Once Upon a Time in Mexico part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Mexico Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
